evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed spring layout clearly shows 4 color-coded communities; edge alpha=0.25 reduces hairball; ring-like backbone across communities is visible.
evidence.typography: Script saves PDF/SVG but does NOT set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; font is matplotlib default (DejaVu Sans), not a publication-quality face; sizes (6–10 pt) are acceptable but small.
evidence.palette: Four-color palette (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) avoids red/green pairing for direction; passes common CVD simulations (deuteranopia-safe); categorical discipline maintained.
evidence.layout_density: 7×7-inch canvas well-filled; no gaping whitespace; node labels readable; legend placed upper-left without obscuring graph; y-range (axis off) appropriate.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; legend present with community labels; title on figure and caption align; no overlapping labels observed.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf and figure.svg are produced but script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or svg.fonttype; text will be rendered as outlines/bitmap, not selectable glyphs.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (brief only required pdf+png).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states node count (30), community count (4), algorithm (spring-embedding, seed 42), edge alpha (0.25), and a structural finding (ring-like backbone); specific and informative.

overall_strengths: Clean, readable community visualization with good palette discipline and tuned edge alpha that avoids hairball clutter.
Caption is precise and includes numerical details (30 nodes, 4 communities, alpha=0.25) plus a concrete scientific observation.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype=0 settings, so text in vector outputs is not editable/selectable — a standard journal requirement.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; a publication figure would typically specify a serif or sans-serif matching the journal style (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a LaTeX-compatible face).
